description: Platform-agnostic base for all Docent platform schemas: the top-level envelope plus the cross-platform $defs (no platform-specific fields or wording). Composed by scripts/build-schemas.js with optional intermediate family layers (e.g. desktop.shared.schema.json) and a per-surface leaf delta into each published platform schema. Not used directly for validation — use the platform-specific schemas.

docent_format.description: Self-describing format stamp identifying which Docent platform and schema version produced this file. Lets any consumer pick the correct schema and route migrations without guessing. The platform and schema_version values are fixed (const) per published schema.

platform.description: The Docent platform that produced this file (set per published schema).

schema_version.description: The schema version this file conforms to (set per published schema).

steps.description: All step version records for this recording. Contains full history including re-recorded and deleted versions.

narration.description: Free-text narration describing the step intent. Present in narration mode.

narration_source.description: How the narration was provided. Written at capture alongside narration; the pairing is not schema-enforced.

step_type.description: Step classification in simple mode. Present in simple mode.

expect.description: Assertion type for validation steps. Present when step_type is 'validation'.

x.description: Pointer X at the action, in the capture surface's coordinate space: the capturing document's viewport for in-page capture, or the screen (physical pixels) for native capture.

y.description: Pointer Y at the action, in the capture surface's coordinate space: the capturing document's viewport for in-page capture, or the screen (physical pixels) for native capture.

x.description: Pointer X at the action, in the capture surface's coordinate space: the capturing document's viewport for in-page capture, or the screen (physical pixels) for native capture.

y.description: Pointer Y at the action, in the capture surface's coordinate space: the capturing document's viewport for in-page capture, or the screen (physical pixels) for native capture.

value.description: The entered value. Sensitive values are masked at capture — passwords, payment fields designated by their autocomplete tokens, and fields whose name/id matches the shared financial/secret/SSN pattern — and the element is flagged redacted.
Visible text of the selected option.

key.description: Key name. Control keys use standard names (Enter, Escape, Tab, ArrowUp, etc.). Printable characters use their character value (A, 1, Space, etc.).

x.description: Pointer X at the action, in the capture surface's coordinate space: the capturing document's viewport for in-page capture, or the screen (physical pixels) for native capture.

y.description: Pointer Y at the action, in the capture surface's coordinate space: the capturing document's viewport for in-page capture, or the screen (physical pixels) for native capture.

source_element.description: The dragged element. Null if drag started before recording.

element.description: The scrolled element. Null for page-level scroll.

tag.description: Element kind or control type (e.g. a button, text input, or link). "unknown" when the element could not be identified.

id.description: Stable, developer-assigned identifier for the element. Null when absent.

name.description: Element name or accessible name. Null when absent.

role.description: Accessibility role of the element. Null when absent.

type.description: Element subtype when applicable, e.g. "password" for masked text inputs. Null otherwise.

autocomplete.description: The field's autocomplete hint when present (e.g. "cc-number"), used to identify sensitive payment fields. Null when absent or not applicable.

position_in_set.description: One-based position of the element within the logical set of related items it belongs to, as reported by the source accessibility layer. The logical set can differ from what is currently rendered. Null or absent when not reported.

size_of_set.description: Number of items in the logical set of related items this element belongs to (the counterpart of position_in_set), as reported by the source accessibility layer. Null or absent when not reported.

level.description: One-based depth of the element within a hierarchical set (e.g. an item's depth in a tree), as reported by the source accessibility layer. Null or absent when not reported.

framework_id.description: Identifier of the UI framework that supplies this element, as reported by the source accessibility layer. Applications can mix frameworks, so this is recorded per element. Null or absent when not reported.

text.description: Visible text, truncated to 100 characters (a truncation mark may be appended). Null for password fields or when empty.

selector.description: Locator for the element: a platform-specific selector or accessibility path, or "coord:x,y" when only coordinates are available.

redacted.description: True when the captured value and/or text was redacted because the field was sensitive (a password, payment, or other PII field). Absent when nothing was redacted.

locators.description: Locator candidates: observed facts about how this element could be addressed at capture time. Each entry is a per-strategy shape; the set of valid strategies is platform-specific (see this schema's locator definition). Entries appear in the fixed order their strategy definitions are declared in the locator definition — that order is a serialization convention and carries no preference or ranking. Omitted when no candidates were observed; candidates whose value was empty are omitted rather than included empty.

described_after_ms.description: Milliseconds between the action's timestamp and the moment this element description (including any locator match measurements) was captured, as observed by the capture layer. 0 means the element was described at the input itself. Larger values mean the description reflects the interface as it stood that long after the input — the action's effects may already have run. Null or absent when not observed.

locator_match_count.description: How many elements this candidate matched, measured under the scope, order, and timing stated by this schema's locator definition. Present only where it was cheap to measure at capture time; absent means not measured, never a guess.

locator_match_index.description: Zero-based position of the acted-on element in this candidate's match list, measured together with match_count (0 <= match_index < match_count; the upper bound is stated here because it cannot be schema-enforced). Null means this candidate did not match the acted-on element at all. Absent means not measured.

locator_masked.description: True when this candidate's value derived from sensitive content and was masked in place (the same mask glyph as other captured values); the entry is kept, never omitted. match_count and match_index were measured against the real value before masking. Absent when nothing was masked.

context_id.description: Identifier for the window, tab, or surface in which the action occurred. Session-scoped; not persistent across restarts.

metadata.description: User-defined key-value pairs. Values can be a string or an array of strings.
